disclaimer: Entry rules can change. Always verify with official immigration or embassy sources before booking.

visa.notes: Spanish passport holders currently receive a 45-day visa exemption for tourism. No visa required on arrival. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your intended departure date. A return or onward ticket may be requested at immigration.
visa.verify_note: Vietnam periodically adjusts visa exemption terms and eligible nationalities. Verify current conditions at the official Vietnam Immigration Department or the Spanish Embassy in Hanoi before booking.

insurance.notes: Not legally required but strongly recommended. Ensure your policy covers: motorbike riding (often excluded by default), medical evacuation, and adventure activities. Public hospitals outside major cities have limited English-speaking staff.

regions.area: North Vietnam (Hanoi, Ha Long Bay, Sapa)
regions.notes: Cool and dry Oct–Apr. Hot and rainy Jun–Sep. Sapa can be very cold in Dec–Jan — pack layers.

regions.area: Central Vietnam (Hoi An, Da Nang, Hue)
regions.notes: Dry season Feb–Jul. Heavy rain and flooding risk Oct–Nov. Typhoon risk Sep–Nov.

regions.area: South Vietnam (Ho Chi Minh City, Mekong Delta, Phu Quoc)
regions.notes: Dry season Nov–Apr. Heavy afternoon downpours throughout May–Oct monsoon season.

budget.verify_note: Rates are approximate. Check xe.com before travel.

budget.label: Backpacker
budget.includes: Dorm bed, street food 3x, local transport, 1 Bia Hoi

mid.label: Comfort
mid.includes: Private guesthouse, mix of restaurants, Grab rides, activities

high.label: Boutique
high.includes: Boutique hotel, restaurants, tours, cooking classes

useful_apps.name: Grab
useful_apps.use: Taxi & food delivery in major cities

useful_apps.name: Google Maps
useful_apps.use: Navigation — download offline maps before arriving

useful_apps.name: 12Go
useful_apps.use: Bus, train & ferry booking across Vietnam

useful_apps.name: Airalo
useful_apps.use: eSIM data — buy before you land

useful_apps.name: Baolau
useful_apps.use: Sleeper bus, train & ferry tickets in Vietnam

transport: Grab works well in Hanoi, Ho Chi Minh City, and Da Nang — always safer and cheaper than unlicensed taxis.
Sleeper buses (giường nằm) connect major cities overnight — quality varies greatly, read reviews before booking on 12Go or Baolau.
Domestic flights save significant time between north and south — book early on VietJet or Bamboo for the lowest fares.
The Reunification Express train is scenic but slow — best for the Hanoi–Da Nang–Ho Chi Minh route when you have time to spare.
Motorbike rental is widespread — carry your international driving permit and photograph all pre-existing damage before riding.
Use 12Go or Baolau to compare and pre-book intercity transport, especially during Tết and peak season.

scams.name: Taxi meter manipulation
scams.description: Some taxis in tourist areas run rigged meters or take deliberately long routes. Use Grab for all city rides to get a fixed price upfront.

scams.name: Fake tour agencies
scams.description: Storefronts near tourist attractions may pose as reputable operators selling Ha Long Bay cruises or Sapa trekking packages. Book through verified operators or well-reviewed hostels.

scams.name: Motorbike damage dispute
scams.description: Rental shops may charge for pre-existing damage on return. Photograph every scratch and dent thoroughly before riding — share photos via message with the owner as a timestamp.

scams.name: Overpriced tourist transport
scams.description: Fixed-price 'tourist' buses sold at storefronts are often 3–5x the going rate. Compare on 12Go or Baolau before paying.

emergency.embassy_label: Spanish Embassy in Hanoi / Consular assistance

embassy.phone: Verify official embassy contact before travel
embassy.address: Verify official embassy contact before travel

emergency.notes: Tourist hotline (1800 599 961) provides English support — verify availability before travel. For emergencies involving Spanish nationals, contact the Spanish Embassy in Hanoi.

checklist.item: Passport valid 6+ months beyond your intended departure date
Verify current 45-day visa exemption at the Vietnam Immigration Department or Spanish Embassy in Hanoi
Purchase travel insurance (check motorbike and medical evacuation coverage)
Print or save a return/onward ticket — may be checked at immigration
Notify your bank of travel dates to avoid card blocks
Download Google Maps offline and install Grab before arriving
Buy Airalo eSIM or arrange a local SIM on arrival at the airport
Bring EUR to exchange on arrival — airport exchange desks are generally fair
Save Vietnam emergency numbers and tourist hotline in your phone
Photograph all motorbike damage before renting — share photos with the owner
Pack layers for Sapa if visiting Dec–Feb — temperatures can drop near freezing
